frameworks.name: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA)
frameworks.one_line: Comparing representational dissimilarity matrices across modalities to assess second-order similarity.

frameworks.name: Multivariate noise normalization (NNM)
frameworks.one_line: Preprocessing step proposed to boost RDM reliability across dissimilarity metrics.

frameworks.name: GLMdenoise
frameworks.one_line: Alternative noise-reduction pipeline benchmarked against NNM for RSA.

core_question: Does multivariate noise normalization reliably improve the reliability of neural RDMs for RSA in fMRI?
key_assumptions: RDM correlations across modalities are meaningful only if RDMs are reliable
Within-subject reliability is a valid criterion for evaluating preprocessing choices
Multiple dissimilarity metrics should respond consistently to a useful preprocessing step
manipulations: Region of interest within the same dataset
Interaction with spatial smoothing and with GLMdenoise
key_findings: Impact of NNM on within- and between-subject RDM reliability was highly variable across three fMRI datasets
Variability persisted across dissimilarity metrics, ROIs, and smoothing levels
NNM did not consistently outperform GLMdenoise
interpretations: NNM's benefits for RSA are not robust and resemble the variability seen with other analysis choices
Authors advise conservatism before adding preprocessing complexity to RSA pipelines
limitations_noted: Tested on three fMRI datasets only; generalization beyond these is unclear
